entry.title: Crumbl Bakes New Triple Berry Shortcake
entry.summary:
Crumbl bakes a new Triple Berry Shortcake as part of this week's limited-time dessert menu from September 29 through October 4, 2025.
Here's what this week's menu looks like: - Triple Berry Shortcake - A two-layer vanilla shortcake with whipped cream, topped with house-made triple berry jam. - Chocolate Peanut Butter Cookie featuring Oreo - A warm chocolate cookie with Oreo cookie pieces and peanut butter chips, topped with a peanut butter mousse and a mini Oreo cookie - Confetti Cake Cookie - A warm sugar cookie with confetti sprinkles, topped with pink vanilla cream cheese frosting, and more confetti sprinkles. - Molten Lava Cookie - A warm, dark chocolate cookie filled with hot fudge and sprinkled with powdered sugar. - Pumpkin Pie Cookie - A chilled, buttery pie crust cookie topped with spiced pumpkin pie filling, a rosette of whipped cream, and a dusting of cinnamon. - Semi-Sweet Chocolate Chunk Cookie - A warm cookie packed with semi-sweet chocolate chunks and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt. - Weekly "Secret Menu" - A mystery cookie or dessert that varies with location. You can check the Crumbl website or mobile app to see what's available near you. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: BJ's Restaurant & Brewhouse Welcomes 2025 Halloween Menu
entry.summary:
BJ's Restaurant & Brewhouse brings back its Spooky Pizookie as part of its seasonal menu ahead of Halloween 2025.
The Spooky Pizookie is a Cookies 'N' Cream cookie topped with Oreo crumbs, Halloween sprinkles, and a stack of two scoops of vanilla bean ice cream. It's served with a "transforming crackable chocolate topping" that you pour on yourself. Also featured on the seasonal menu are: - Unlucky Ducky - A cocktail made with Altos Plata Tequila,| Malibu Coconut Rum, Absolut Mandrin Vodka, Bols Peach Schnapps, Bols Triple Sec, Monin Sweet and Sour, pineapple juice, Starry, and fresh lemon, served in a mini bathtub with a rubber ducky. - Blueberry Sour - Maker’s Mark Bourbon, Monin Sweet and Sour, Monin Blood Orange, Monin Agave, blueberries, fresh lemon juice, and dried blood orange. - Orange Scream-Sicle - A non-alcoholic beverage featuring BJ’s Handcrafted Orange Cream Soda mixed with Monin Blood Orange, and cream, topped with whipped cream, a fresh raspberry "nose", mint "hair," and "spooky" gummy eyes. - Sweet & Spicy Asian Glazed Wings - A new "signature" sauce for boneless and bone-in wings. - Dill Pickle Ranch Wings - A new "signature" dry rub for boneless and bone-in wings. - Brewhouse Bites Sampler - A tray of Sliders, Mozzarella Sticks, Bone-In or Boneless Wings, and Chips with a choice of Spinach & Artichoke Dip, Housemade Guacamole, or Sriracha Queso Dip. The casual dining chain also brings back its Oktoberfest Beer, a "smooth, traditional Oktoberfest-style amber lager," for a limited time. Photo via BJ's Restauran & Brewhouse. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: New Double Chicken Burrito Bowls Arrive at El Pollo Loco
entry.summary:
El Pollo Loco launches new Double Chicken Burrito Bowls in two varieties for a limited time this fall.
There's the Queso Crunch Double Chicken Burrito Bowl and the Street Corn Double Chicken Burrito Bowl. The Queso Crunch Double Chicken Burrito Bowl (pictured) features pinto beans and seasoned rice, topped with warm queso blanco, a double portion of citrus-marinated flame-grilled chicken, shredded jack cheese, handmade guacamole, chopped cilantro, and salsa fresca. Tortilla strips are included on the side. The Street Corn Double Chicken Burrito Bowl is inspired by elotes and consists of seasoned rice and pinto beans, topped with a double portion of citrus-marinated flame-grilled chicken, creamy chipotle sauce, handmade guacamole, shredded jack cheese, crumbled cotija, roasted corn, roasted red bell peppers, chopped cilantro, and salsa fresca. Prices vary, but my local El Pollo Loco in Los Angeles charges $12.79 for each bowl. El Pollo Loco is also offering the Fam Feast as a limited-time, discounted group meal exclusively to its rewards members. The Fam Feast includes eight pieces of citrus-marinated flame-grilled chicken, three large sides, eight warm flour or corn tortillas (or a large order of tortilla chips), and four churros. Prices vary, but it costs $29.99 near me with legs and thighs ($34.49 for mixed chicken pieces). Photo via El Pollo Loco. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Review: Little Caesars - Ultimate Cheesesteak Pizza
entry.summary:
Little Caesars' Ultimate Cheesesteak Pizza features a large round original crust topped with a cheesy white sauce, mozzarella, seasoned sliced steak, onions, green peppers, and mushrooms.
I bought one for $16.99. The price is more than any listed pizza on the menu (the next closest in price is the 5 Meat Feast for $14.99).
The pizza hit the cheesesteak theme very well. Between the mozzarella and the cheesy white sauce, it was very cheesy with a nice stretch and a surprising buttery note.
The steak wasn't abundant or spread evenly, but it offered a tender bite and good, lightly seasoned meatiness.
The veggies were also good. They were just cooked through, so they weren't mushy and still had a decent bite. Additionally, they had a strong but not overpowering presence taste-wise, particularly the mushrooms. Just a note: Little Caesars in California uses fresh mushrooms, given the proximity to all the farms in Central California. That is not necessarily the case in other states, so your mileage may vary. The crust was okay. It was sturdy with some chew to it and some crisping here and there.
Overall, Little Caesars' Ultimate Cheesesteak Pizza hit the nail on the head as far as cheesesteak pizzas go. The flavors and textures hit the right notes. However, while the cheesiness was great, I would have liked at more beef (I get that steak is expensive, but wonder if ground beef would have worked). Also, the price is a bit steep for what I got. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: IHOP Launches New Coffee Cake Pancakes
entry.summary:
IHOP introduces new Coffee Cake Pancakes as a limited-time Spotlight Stack flavor.
The new flavor features buttermilk pancakes filled with cinnamon streusel, layered with cinnamon spread, and topped with cream cheese icing, more streusel, and whipped topping. Prices vary, but a short stack (two pancakes) costs $9.49 at my local IHOP in Los Angeles, while a full stack (four pancakes) costs $13.99. A short stack is also available as part of a breakfast combo that also includes two eggs, two strips of bacon or pork sausage links, and hash browns for $17.49. The "Spotlight Stack" is what IHOP is now calling its limited-time pancake flavors. Previously, the chain was going with "Pancake of the Month," which probably made it harder to keep a popular flavor around longer than a month. The diner chain is also serving limited-time Pumpkin Spice Pancakes for the fall. Photo via IHOP. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Charleys Cheesesteaks Puts Together New $10.99 Xtreme Meal Deal
entry.summary:
As an exclusive for its rewards members, Charleys Cheesesteaks offers the limited-time Xtreme Meal Deal for $10.99.
For the price, you get a regular Chicken Philly or Philly Cheesesteak, any regular-sized fries (choose from Louisiana Cajun Fries, Cheese Fries, Cheese & Bacon Fries, and Ultimate Fries), and a regular-sized Real Fruit Lemonade (options include Original, Watermelon Strawberry, Strawberry, and Peach lemonades). The deal is available for use daily in-store and online for Charleys Rewards members. The regular price for the same combo ranges from $17.97 to $19.87 at the nearest Charleys location here in Los Angeles. Photo via Charleys Cheesesteaks. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: KFC Serving a Pickled Menu in Canada
entry.summary:
KFC is serving up the new, limited-time Pickled Menu in Canada, and it includes Pickle Pepsi among other pickle-themed items.
Here's what you'll find on the KFC Pickled Menu:
- Pickle Pepsi - It's Pepsi cola served with a side of pickle slices in pickle brine that you can combine as you like. - Pickle Sandwich - A triple-breaded fried chicken filet served with crispy pickle chips, mayo, bread & butter pickles, and a dusting of dill seasoning, on a toasted potato bun. - Loaded Pickle Fries - Seasoned fries topped with crispy pickle chips, mayo, and dill pickle seasoning. - Fried Pickle Chips- Breaded and fried dill pickle chips served with a side of ranch for dipping. Here in the States, we only got the Fried Pickle Chips during the summer, although they did offer a Pickle Fried Chicken Sandwich here at one point. Photo via KFC Canada. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.summary:
Pumpkin Spice Cinnamon Rolls are back at Cicis Pizza for fall 2025.
Available for a limited time, the seasonal dessert takes the chain's regular cinnamon rolls and drizzles them with a pumpkin spice-infused icing (with notes of cinnamon, clove, and nutmeg) instead of regular icing. You can find them at the buffet or order them for takeout or delivery. Prices vary, but a location in Dallas sells an 8-piece order for $4.99 and a 20-piece order for $7.99. Photo via Cicis Pizza. entry.title: IHOP Introduces New Everyday Value Menu
entry.summary:
IHOP follows up on its weekday-only value menu with a new everyday value menu, featuring four breakfast meals for $6 ($7 in some areas) for a limited time at participating locations nationwide every day from 7AM to 10PM.
The items on the value menu include: - Breakfast Faves Combo - Two buttermilk pancakes, two eggs, and two bacon strips or two pork sausage links. - Ham & Cheese Omelette - An omelette made with ham, jack cheese, cheddar cheese, and white cheese sauce, served with two buttermilk pancakes. - French Toast Faves Combo - A slice of Classic Thick ‘N Fluffy French Toast, two bacon strips or two pork sausage links, and two eggs. - House Scramble - Eggs scrambled with chopped hickory-smoked bacon, jack cheese, and cheddar cheese, served with a side of hash browns. If you're following along at home, the new value menu is the previous House Faves value menu, only now available during the weekends. Photo via IHOP. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Panera's 2025 Fall Menu Arrives
entry.summary:
Fall arrives at Panera with the rollout of the bakery cafe chain's 2025 fall menu. This year's seasonal menu features the return of Black Bean Soup after seven years, the addition of a couple of new menu items, and more.
Here's what you'll find on the menu at Panera this fall: - New Harvest Medley Chicken Salad - Mixed greens and fresh arugula tossed in balsamic vinaigrette, topped with a whole grain blend (red rice and farro), grilled chicken, mandarin oranges, feta, dried cranberries, pumpkin seeds, toasted pecans, and beet strips. - New Creme Brulee Latte - Espresso mixed with foamed milk and naturally-flavored creme brulee syrup, topped with whipped cream and salted caramel sugar. - Black Bean Soup - A hearty vegetarian soup featuring black beans, onion, celery, red bell pepper, carrots, lima beans, potatoes, lemon juice, and garlic. - Autumn Squash Soup - Butternut squash and pumpkin blended with vegetable broth, honey, apple juice, and a hint of curry spices, finished with sweet cream and topped with roasted pumpkin seeds. - Hearty Fireside Chili - Beef, kidney beans, fire-roasted tomatoes, and green chili peppers, simmered in a broth with chili spices and cilantro. - Pumpkin Cookie - A pumpkin-shaped shortbread decorated with orange and green icing. In addition to the fall menu, Panera continues to offer Ciabatta Dippers for a limited time. Photo via Panera. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Review: Taco Bell - Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos
entry.summary:
Taco Bell's Slow-Roasted Chicken Nachos Grande features a double portion of slow-roasted chicken (compared to the Cantina Chicken Soft Taco), refried beans, warm nacho cheese sauce, a three-cheese blend (mozzarella, pepper jack, and cheddar), pico de gallo, reduced-fat sour cream, and guacamole, served over a bed of tortilla chips.
It came in a box and cost me $6.99. The box was filled pretty well with a decent amount of chicken, toppings, and chips. Topping coverage was pretty good, although I always find myself wishing for just a little more nacho cheese sauce.
As with a lot of things at Taco Bell, the combination of flavors was very familiar, as the Grande Nachos aren't much different from Taco Bell's previous nachos in a box (or regular menu Nachos BellGrande, for that matter). Compared to Nachos BellGrande, you get more food for a similar price ($5.99 for the seasoned beef version of both at my local Taco Bell). I would guess it to be a third more food. It's definitely shareable. What stood out from the Grande Nachos over Nachos BellGrande was the guacamole and pico de gallo, which added an acidity that the latter lacks (it has sour cream and tomatoes, but they don't add much). The chicken was also interesting, as Taco Bell's limited-time nachos usually come with beef (either seasoned ground beef or steak). The slow-roasted chicken was tender and meaty with a nice seasoning to it. I much prefer it to the chain's previous chicken, which was milder in flavor and less satisfying texture-wise.
Overall, Taco Bell's Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos get a thumbs up from me. While familiar, there was a nice variety of flavors and textures to go with a good amount of food for the money. Nutritional Info - Taco Bell - Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos Calories - 1100 Fat - 59g (Saturated Fat - 9g) Sodium - 1720mg Carbs - 111g (Sugar - 6g) Protein - 32g Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Jack in the Box Brings Back the Sourdough Scrambler
entry.summary:
The Sourdough Scrambler makes a limited-time return to Jack in the Box as part of the chain's all-day breakfast menu.
The breakfast sandwich features scrambled eggs, two slices of melted American cheese, and a choice of hickory-smoked bacon strips or a grilled sausage patty on toasted sourdough rounds (the same as you'd get with the Sourdough Jack). You can find my review of the sausage version here. In addition to a la carte, the Sourdough Scrambler is available in a combo with hot coffee and a hash brown. The price on Jack in the Box's Sourdough Scrambler starts at $2.99 (it costs $3.49 near me in Los Angeles). The combo meal costs $5.99 near me. Photo via Jack in the Box. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.summary:
The Trick or Treat Blast is back at Sonic for the 2025 Halloween season and is available now through the Sonic mobile app ahead of a national launch on September 29, 2025.
The Trick or Treat Blast features a choice of vanilla or chocolate soft serve mixed with Heath candy bar pieces, M&M's Minis, and Oreo cookie pieces, topped off with more of the same candies and cookies. Prices vary by location, with an average price of $6.39 for a medium. Photo via Sonic. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Carl's Jr. Testing New Really Big Dill Burger
entry.summary:
Carl's Jr. is testing the new Really Big Dill Burger for a limited time at participating locations in Central California.
Carl's Jr.'s Really Big Dill Burger (not to be confused with Sonic's Big Dill Cheeseburger) features a charbroiled beef patty, dill aioli sauce, dill pickle fries, pickle crisps, melted Swiss cheese, lettuce, and tomato served on a toasted sesame seed bun. It is also available with two or three beef patties. The Dill pickle fries can also be ordered as a side. Pickles are a trending ingredient/flavor right now, and several fast-food chains have embraced the trend, including Sonic (with a pickle menu), Popeyes (also with a pickle menu), Del Taco (with a fried pickle taco), and more. Photo via Carl's Jr. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Jersey Mike's Getting Rid of Portabella Mushrooms
entry.summary:
Jersey Mike's is in the process of removing portabella mushrooms from its menu. Some locations have already used up the last of their stock, and the mushrooms will no longer be available chain-wide by early October 2025.
The removal of portabella mushrooms affects sandwiches #64 to #66, i.e., the Grilled Portabella Mushroom & Swiss (pictured), the Portabella Cheese Steak, and the Portabella Chicken Cheese Steak. If you're a vegetarian, that leaves The Veggie (#14) as the chain's remaining vegetarian sub. Portabella mushrooms were first offered at Jersey Mike's in March 2021. Photo via Jersey Mike's. entry.title: Culver's Reveals New Jalapeno Cheese Curds and Return of the CurderBurger
entry.summary:
In celebration of its first Curdtoberfest (like Oktoberfest but honoring cheese curds), Culver's reveals new Jalapeno Cheese Curds and the return of the CurderBurger.
Jalapeno Cheese Curds (pictured) will arrive on October 15, 2025 (apparently, the 10th anniversary of National Cheese Curd Day) and will be available for a limited time through October 31, 2025, while supplies last. They're a spicy take on Culver's classic Wisconsin Cheese Curds made with bits of jalapeno pepper and cheddar cheese. The CurderBurger features a Deluxe ButterBurger topped with a breaded and fried patty of cheese (a blend of yellow and white cheddar). It arrives on September 29, 2025, and will be available through October 14, 2025, while supplies last. It first started as an April Fools' joke in 2021, but then they made it into an actual burger, and this is the fifth time they've brought it back. In addition to the limited-time menu items, Culver's first-ever Curdtoberfest will include themed decor at its restaurants and an online sweepstakes with "free Wisconsin Cheese Curds for a year" and other prizes. Photo via Culver's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Moe's Southwest Grill Puts Together New $9.95 Value Meal
entry.summary:
Moe's Southwest Grill debuts the new Moe Value Meal, a discounted combo meal that includes a junior burrito, a side of queso, and a fountain drink for $9.95. As with Moe's other entrees, you also get free chips, salsa, and dipping sauces.
To promote the new offering, Moe's is holding a sweepstakes game, the Moe Value Meal Game Show, with a grand prize of a year of Moe Value Meals, 1,000 prizes of a free Moe Value Meal, and a reward code for 20% off a Moe Value Meal for all participants. You must be a Moe Rewards member to redeem any of the prizes. The game can be found online at www.moevaluemeal.com and runs through October 5, 2025. Photo via Moe's Southwest Grill. entry.title: Carl's Jr. Now Serves Its Chicken Sandwiches on Sourdough Bread
entry.summary:
In an odd bit of a switch, Carl's Jr. now serves almost all of its charbroiled and hand-breaded chicken sandwiches on toasted sourdough bread instead of a bun. The only exception is the value-priced Spicy Chicken Sandwich.
Items now served on sourdough include: - Hand-Breaded Chicken Sandwich (pictured) - Hand-Breaded Bacon Swiss Chicken Sandwich - Charbroiled Chicken Club Sandwich - Charbroiled Santa Fe Chicken Sandwich - Charbroiled BBQ Chicken Sandwich Given that fast food sourdough tends not to be sour, the switch amounts to serving its chicken sandwiches on toast. entry.title: Crumbl Bakes Hazelnut Sea Salt Cookies and More Through September 27, 2025
entry.summary:
Crumbl has revealed its limited-time menu for this week, and it includes Hazelnut Sea Salt Cookies and more. The cookie bakery chain celebrates its birthday this week by offering a deal where you can get a 6-pack of cookies for the price of a 4-pack on Wednesday, September 24, 2025.
Here's what you can expect to find on the Crumbl menu from September 22 through September 27, 2025: - Hazelnut Sea Salt Cookie - A warm browned butter cookie stuffed with hazelnut spread and topped with a drizzle of more hazelnut spread and a sprinkle of sea salt. - Confetti Cake - A two-tier confetti cake layered with vanilla buttercream and topped with a sprinkle of rainbow sprinkles. - Birthday Cake Cookie featuring Oreo - A warm cookie made with Oreo cookie pieces, topped with cake batter cream cheese frosting, more Oreo cookie pieces, and rainbow sprinkles. - Original Pink Sugar Cookie - A chilled almond sugar cookie topped with pink almond frosting. - Milk Chocolate Chip Cookie - A warm cookie packed with milk chocolate chips. Additionally, each location has two "secret menu" cookies this week. You can check the Crumbl website or mobile app to see what's being offered near you. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!
